summary:
Perfect! The test now passes. The fix was to check the idempotency key **before** making any database mutations (debiting the source account). This prevents the race condition where multiple concurrent requests could all debit the source account before any of them records the idempotency key.

The key changes:
1. Moved idempotency check to the beginning of the function
2. Removed the rollback logic for the debit since we no longer debit before checking
3. Updated the IntegrityError handler to also undo the credit (since we now credit before the INSERT that might fail)
4. Changed to a single commit at the end after all operations succeed

This ensures that only one request with a given idempotency key can successfully complete the transfer, even under concurrent retry scenarios.
